---
title: "Sampling"
author: "nbanda25"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(primer.data)
```

A census measures every unit in a population — and it is usually too expensive, and often impossible: any population that reaches into the future can never be fully counted. So almost all of data science works instead from a sample and reasons back to the population. How much we can trust that reasoning is the subject of this tutorial.

```{r}
truth <- mean(shaming$age, na.rm = TRUE)
truth
```
